title: Math 314 Final Practice
description: Linear algebra practice covering vector spaces, linear transformations, eigenvalues, and diagonalization.

questions.prompt: Which of the following statements about the set {u, v, w} is TRUE?

choices.label: They are orthogonal and form a basis for R^3

questions.explanation: The vectors are mutually orthogonal and nonzero, so they are independent and form a basis for R^3.

questions.prompt: Given rref(A) has pivots in columns 1 and 2, what must be true about vectors v1, v2, v3, v4?

choices.label: v1 and v2 are independent; v3 and v4 depend on them

questions.explanation: Pivot columns correspond to independent vectors; non-pivot columns are linear combinations.

questions.prompt: If three vectors in R^4 are linearly independent, what is true about their span?

choices.label: They span a 3D subspace of R^4

questions.explanation: Three independent vectors in R^4 span a 3-dimensional subspace, not all of R^4.

questions.prompt: A 3x3 matrix has two eigenvalues and each eigenspace is 1-dimensional. Is it diagonalizable?

choices.label: No, because it lacks enough eigenvectors

questions.explanation: A 3x3 matrix needs 3 independent eigenvectors; here there are only 2.

questions.prompt: If a 7x6 matrix has nullity 4, what is its rank?
questions.explanation: Rank-nullity theorem: rank + nullity = number of columns (6). So rank = 2.

questions.prompt: If W is spanned by two independent vectors in R^5, what is dim(W^⊥)?
Why is a set of 4 vectors in R^3 always linearly dependent?

choices.label: Too many vectors for the dimension

questions.explanation: You cannot have more than 3 independent vectors in R^3.

questions.prompt: What is the geometric interpretation of a 2D subspace in R^3?
questions.explanation: A 2-dimensional subspace in R^3 is a plane through the origin.

questions.prompt: What does it mean for a linear transformation to be injective?
questions.explanation: Injective means one-to-one, which occurs when the null space is trivial.

choices.label: When it has n independent eigenvectors

questions.explanation: A matrix is diagonalizable if it has a basis of eigenvectors.
